// Fixture mimicking the EVENTS array shape from
// code.claude.com/docs/en/context-window.md.
// The page exports EVENTS as a typed array of objects describing turn
// events visualized in the context-window timeline.

export const EVENTS = [
  {
    t: 0,
    kind: 'system',
    label: 'Session start',
    tokens: 1200,
    color: '#666',
    vis: 'visible',
    desc: 'Opening system prompt + tool catalog',
  },
  {
    t: 1,
    kind: 'user',
    label: 'User turn 1',
    tokens: 80,
    color: '#1d72b8',
    vis: 'visible',
    desc: 'First user message',
  },
  {
    t: 2,
    kind: 'tool',
    label: 'Read file',
    tokens: 1500,
    color: '#888',
    vis: 'visible',
    desc: 'Output of Read tool',
    link: 'https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/tool-use/overview',
  },
  {
    t: 3,
    kind: 'assistant',
    label: 'Assistant turn',
    tokens: 200,
    color: '#10b981',
    vis: 'visible',
    desc: 'Reasoning + tool call',
    link: null,
  },
  {
    t: 4,
    kind: 'compaction',
    label: 'Auto-compaction',
    tokens: -3000,
    color: '#ef4444',
    vis: 'hidden',
    desc: 'Compaction event invoked',
  },
];

# Context window

Some prose around the export — `subagent-html` would extract this; we don't.

The data above is what `subagent-js` reads.
